title: Guinea Detains 11 JNIM Suspects in Eastern Border Operation
detail: Guinean armed forces arrested 11 individuals suspected of links to JNIM cells during a counter-terrorism operation in the east of the country, in border areas adjacent to Mali. The operation was launched in response to a series of JNIM militant attacks on the Malian-Guinean border that had prompted Conakry to deploy additional military units to reinforce border security. Authorities described these as the first active measures against JNIM since the group's presence in Guinea was first recorded in 2024. The activation of JNIM in these border areas has been flagged as a national security risk due to the group's use of the region for logistics, recruitment, and attack preparation.

title: Malian Air Force Strikes Militant Positions in Central Mali
detail: The Malian Air Force conducted airstrikes against militant positions in central Mali, targeting armed groups in the Mopti region. The operations are part of an ongoing FAMa push to clear territory following the expiry of the JNIM fuel blockade truce negotiations. The strikes follow the ground operations of March 22 in Sikasso and Ségou and reflect FAMa's increasing use of air power — supported by African Corps (formerly Wagner) air assets — to disrupt jihadist mobility in the interior.
